$comment: Final post plan for the FB Page 14-day warm-up. Consumed by scripts/schedule-warmup-posts.mjs. Image paths are relative to repo root. Day offset is from --start-date CLI arg. 'time' is local time in the timezone passed via --tz. 'captions.primary' fires on the schedule; 'variants' are kept in library for future rotation (not scheduled automatically).

captions.primary:
Hey — I'm Alec. I started little color book because the best photos on my phone were all just sitting there.

The idea's simple. You pick a favorite photo. We turn it into a clean, black-and-white coloring page your kid can actually color themselves — or their dog, their grandma, their cousins — whoever's in the shot.

A screen-free activity for tonight. A keepsake worth keeping for later.

A free sample is live at littlecolorbook.com if you want to try one. I'd love to see what you make.
variants:
Hi — I'm Alec. little color book started with one thought: the best photos on my phone were just sitting there, and my kid would rather color than scroll.

So we built this. Pick a favorite photo. We turn it into a clean coloring page. Your kid colors themselves (or the dog, or grandma) onto the page. That's it.

A free sample is up at littlecolorbook.com. Would love to see what you make.

Hey folks — founder of little color book here.

Honestly? I was tired of photo books no one opens. I wanted something our kid would actually touch. So we made this: favorite photos, turned into coloring pages a kid can color themselves onto.

Free sample at littlecolorbook.com. One photo, one page, no account — I'd love your feedback.

little color book turns favorite photos into personalized coloring pages kids actually want to use. Upload a photo, we send back a clean, printable page.

That's little color book — personalized coloring pages made from the photos already on your phone. Free sample at littlecolorbook.com.

posts.purpose: How it works — simple narrative in single-image form (was carousel in draft; simplified for Graph API scheduling)

captions.primary:
How little color book works, in 4 small steps:

1. Pick a favorite photo from your camera roll.
2. We turn it into a clean, printable coloring page.
3. Your kid colors themselves (or the dog, or grandma) onto the page.
4. Keep it on the fridge, frame it, or order a spiral-bound book.

Free sample at littlecolorbook.com.
variants:
Photo → coloring page → an hour of quiet → a keepsake.

That's the flow. Upload one favorite photo, we send back a clean page your kid can actually color. Print it tonight or save it for a birthday.

littlecolorbook.com
